reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 3 gates passed. Gate 1 skipped (missing-validation type: issue is that forceApprove(oldAuction, 0) is NOT called in _setAuction). Gate 2: exploit execution is by the old auction contract (untrusted external actor) calling ERC20.transferFrom — not a trusted data source. Gate 3: src/AuctionBidder.sol is in scope; stale approval logic is not a known-limitation non-canonical ERC20 issue. Severity held at Medium: conditional loss of funds requiring (a) residual approval from bid()/auctionCallback() where auction consumed less than approved, and (b) old auction being adversarial/compromised post-migration. An attacker can trigger this via the old auction contract by calling ERC20.transferFrom(auctionBidder, attacker, residualAllowance) after a setAuction() migration, causing direct token theft from AuctionBidder up to the residual allowance amount.
